findings.title: pattern-scan.sh grep alternation patterns fail on macOS
findings.why_it_matters: The pattern scanner uses basic regex grep patterns with \| alternation operators to detect Controller annotations, Service annotations, parameter bindings, query methods, entity annotations, and exception handlers. On macOS (BSD grep), BRE does not support alternation with \|, so these patterns are treated as literal strings and will fail to match. This causes the generated patterns.md to miss or under-report most project patterns, defeating the purpose of the scan.
evidence: CLAU DE.md specifies Platform: macOS (darwin), Shell: zsh; BSD grep is the default.
findings.suggested_fix: Add the -E flag to all grep commands that use alternation (\|) in scripts/pattern-scan.sh. For line 3613, also replace \s with [[:space:]] since \s is not portable in BRE.

findings.title: check-gate-2-exit.sh misses build.gradle.kts in report
findings.why_it_matters: The generated VERIFICATION.md detects the build tool by checking for pom.xml or build.gradle, but omits build.gradle.kts. For Gradle projects using the Kotlin DSL, the report incorrectly shows '未知' as the build tool, which is confusing and inconsistent with detect-stack.sh and the script's own suggestion logic that both handle build.gradle.kts.
evidence: Line 3289: elif [ -f "$WORKTREE_DIR/build.gradle" ]; then echo "Gradle"; else echo "未知"; fi
detect-stack.sh lines 3397-3400: checks both build.gradle and build.gradle.kts
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 3289 to: elif [ -f "$WORKTREE_DIR/build.gradle" ] || [ -f "$WORKTREE_DIR/build.gradle.kts" ]; then echo "Gradle"; else echo "未知"; fi

findings.title: check-gate-2-exit.sh AC extraction regex never matches PRD format
findings.why_it_matters: The script attempts to extract acceptance criteria from PRD.md using grep -E '^- \[.*\] AC-[0-9]+:', but the PRD.md template and all samples use '### AC-001:' headers with EARS format (Given/When/Then), not checklist items. Because the regex never matches, the script always falls back to generic placeholder criteria in VERIFICATION.md, requiring manual correction every time.
evidence: tests/fixtures/sample-feature/PRD.md uses the same EARS header format, not checklists
findings.suggested_fix: Update the extraction logic to parse the EARS format under '### AC-XXX:' headers, or extract the AC titles and convert them to checklist items.

residual_risks: pattern-scan.sh uses find -exec grep -l on every .java file; for very large codebases this may be slow, but it is functionally correct.
check-gate-2-exit.sh fallback compliance parsing (grep -oE BLOCK.*[0-9]+) is heuristic and could mis-parse reports that lack the expected front matter.
Gate scripts require exactly D-[0-9]{3} and T-[0-9]{3} formats; non-conforming IDs are silently ignored.
testing_gaps: Integration tests verify pattern-scan.sh produces output files but do not assert the accuracy of extracted counts (e.g., annotation frequencies, query method counts).
test-full-flow.sh does not exercise check-gate-2-exit.sh in any scenario (pass or fail).
No cross-platform test coverage exists for the shell scripts on both GNU and BSD grep/sed.
